key_insight: IGA-ODIL replaces neural-network PINN parameterizations with smooth B-spline/isogeometric bases, making residual minimization faster, sparser, and better conditioned for both forward and inverse PDE problems.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical surrogate-solving route for solid mechanics and PDE-based engineering models when PINNs are too slow or unstable to optimize.
method: Represent unknown fields with B-spline basis functions instead of neural networks.
Minimize robust residual losses using weighted Gram operators so the loss better reflects true error.
Use sparse, structured Gauss-Newton optimization enabled by isogeometric analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports benchmark tests on Poisson, advection-diffusion, Helmholtz, Allen–Cahn, and inverse Helmholtz problems, with orders-of-magnitude speedups over PINNs and CRVPINNs while preserving high accuracy.
watch_item: Performance depends on robust Gauss-Newton optimization and spline discretization quality, so difficult geometries, poor initial guesses, or ill-conditioned inverse setups may still require careful tuning.
paper_title: IGA-ODIL: Optimizing DIscretre robust Loss with Isogeometric Analysis to solve forward and inverse problems faster using machine learning tools
